parsed_review.summary: CP00-873 (RP29 Commercial Readiness, Risk C, 150 units, RP29.P00.M00.S01-RP29.P01.M05.S13) is a descriptor-only contract/domain-foundation pack and passes hardened read-only review. Pack positioning is legitimate: closeout-pack-plan.json live_state shows last-closed CP00-872 with next_subphase RP29.P00.M00.S01 (this pack's first unit) and the source_ledger_sha matches the baseline. Implementation (packages/commercial/src/{model,states,validators,registry,index}.js) declares 5 implemented RP29 models plus a CustomerPlan contract shell, all frozen, tenant-scoped, Matter-trace-required, and AuditEvent-referenced; validators reject any runtime/write/leak flag set true and the CustomerPlan-not-implemented invariant holds. Tests (test/model.test.js) cover coverage-vs-live-plan, runtime-closed descriptor, tenant/audit traceability, contract projection currency, and the compatibility alias, and are wired into root npm test plus rp29:commercial:validate. Safety boundaries are intact: COMMERCIAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and every one of the 150 case-set rows set descriptor_only true and all runtime/creation/write/receipt/real-data/credential flags false; a security grep surfaced only false attestations and guard names (no secrets or real client data). Authority boundary is correct: contract and descriptor assert claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; the Hermes evidence packet sets emits_runtime_receipt=false and the Claude packet read_only=true with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false. Manifest, construction-inspection, adjudication, and command-evidence all correctly hold production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false pending this review, so nothing prematurely claims production, runtime, or enterprise trust. Review-evidence hygiene is sound: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid-JSON result), and valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts are both empty, so no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/tool-shaped attempt is counted as valid evidence. Provenance: file-level consistency is directly verified through read-only tools; the validation command exit codes recorded in command-evidence.json are agent-reported (Bash unavailable to the reviewer) and the full deterministic validation suite (npm test, validate, spec/weighted/fullplan/goal/control-plane validators, git diff --check) runs post-review per the standard closeout flow. No P0-P3 findings; nothing in the static evidence blocks pack or CP closeout.
